mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent trend forces higher real-rate expectations and multiple compression, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return after triggers is indistinguishable from zero at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and lower cyclical demand, which should reduce earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of unemployment-spike episodes, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS and HOUST identifies weakening final demand and housing-linked spending, which should depress sales expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no out-of-sample lift versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real activity without inflation pressure, supporting margin resilience and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not outperform baseline over 60 days, or if the joint signal has a negative sign in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in PAYEMS growth after a prior weak patch signals labor-income stabilization and stronger consumption, which should drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change turns positive after at least 2 consecutive weak monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS recovery events are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for broad market drift.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already low and CPIAUCSL is still subdued, the market reads policy as accommodative and inflation as contained, creating a positive S&P 500 return impulse over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate/low-inflation state does not outperform unconditional 5-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening environment, a simultaneous rise in UNRATE and soft PAYEMS growth signals imminent earnings downgrades and should cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor weakness and tight policy state fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if out-of-sample hit rate stays below 45% in tightening regimes.
